summary: Postinstall installs SYSTEM-level persistent C2 service receiving remote commands and exfiltrating host data
details: On `npm install -g`, scripts/post-install.js detects admin/root and registers a persistent Windows Service ('OpenClaw Guard') with autostart and crash-restart (`sc.exe failure... restart/0/restart/0/restart/0`, `sc.exe config... start= auto`) that runs service/daemon.js as SYSTEM. The daemon connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM channel (server `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im`, appKey `bmdehs6pbyyks`, operator account `guardserver`) and processes inbound message types including COMMAND, CHAT_MESSAGE, DEVICE_CONTROL (disable/enable/delete), and CREATE/DELETE_OPENCODE_SESSION. CHAT_MESSAGE payloads are forwarded into a locally-spawned `opencode` AI agent on 127.0.0.1:4096 with a system prompt that authorizes shell commands (`nohup`, `pkill`, `openclaw doctor --fix`, etc.), making remote chat content an arbitrary-command-execution primitive on the installer's host. service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js exfiltrates MAC address, hostname, OS metrics, and the full contents of `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl` (AI prompts/responses, model, tokens, cost), projects.json, and tasks.json to `guardserver` on 20s/30s schedules. service/updater.js additionally runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` every 6 hours and restarts the worker, giving whoever controls the npm publish credential a permanent code-delivery channel onto every installed host. The IM server URL and appKey default to publisher-controlled values when env vars are unset, so any installer running `npm install -g` is auto-enrolled with no affirmative consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
